bullet_points: Load the dataset and group by oscillator_id.
Extract ground truth physical parameters (m, b, omega, A, phi, zeta) and observed time-series data.
Implement a vectorized function to compute the theoretical energy E_model(t; m_tilde, b_tilde) based on the analytical solution for damped harmonic oscillators.
Calculate the baseline residual at true parameters to establish a "noise floor" for each oscillator.
Perform a sanity check by comparing the theoretical energy at ground truth parameters against the provided total_energy field to ensure physical consistency.
Save the processed parameters, baseline residuals, and validation metrics to a file for subsequent steps.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Data preprocessing, baseline extraction, and theoretical model validation.

bullet_points: Generate a 10x10 grid of perturbed parameters (m_tilde, b_tilde) within +/- 10% of the ground truth for each oscillator.
Compute the 4D array of relative energy residuals Delta E_rel across the parameter grid.
Estimate sensitivity gradients nabla_(m_tilde, b_tilde) Delta E_rel using a central difference scheme.
Compute the rank correlation between the gradient magnitudes and the damping_ratio.
Generate 2D heatmaps of the energy residual manifold for the oscillators with the lowest and highest damping ratios.
Save all calculated metrics and plots.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Parameter perturbation, sensitivity manifold calculation, and correlation analysis.

bullet_points: Interpret the sensitivity gradients and the correlation analysis to assess the robustness of the energy dissipation model.

Compare the observed "noise floor" (from Step 1) against the magnitude of residuals induced by the +/- 10% parameter perturbations to establish a quantitative threshold where parameter uncertainty dominates measurement noise.
Discuss the non-linearities observed in the energy residual manifolds and their relationship with damping regimes.
Synthesize the findings into a comprehensive Results section, referencing the generated plots and statistical summaries.
Provide a final assessment on how physical parameter precision impacts long-term state prediction in underdamped systems.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Synthesis of results and final reporting.
